Kiindulási alapul vedd ezt a cikket:
https://qubit.hu/2023/04/18/hogyan-segithet-a-mesterseges-intelligencia-abban-hogy-minel-kevesebben-bukjanak-ki-a-magyar-egyetemekrol
írj róla egykét bekezdéses összefoglalót.

A magyar egyetemek körében kiemelten fontos, hogy minél kevesebb hallgató veszítse el szükségtelenül az ösztöndíjas helyét. Ezt segítheti elő a mesterséges intelligencia által vezérelt program, amely a Budapesti Műszaki és Gazdaságtudományi Egyetem (BME) kutatóinak és diákjainak együttműködésében jött létre. Ennek során a mesterséges intelligencia elemzi az egyetemi hallgatók eddigi tanulmányi eredményeit, és az alapján jelzéseket küld a tanároknak, hogy mely diákokat kellene különös figyelemmel kísérni.

A cél az, hogy a személyre szabott támogatással csökkenjen a bukási arány és a hallgatók motiváltabbak maradjanak. A program a Tanulmányi Előrehaladást Jelező Rendszer (TEJES) névre hallgat, és a mesterséges intelligencia képes felismerni a veszélyeztetett hallgatók tanulmányi szokásait és eredményeit, így lehetővé téve a megfelelő támogatást számukra. Ez az eszköz lehetőséget biztosít arra, hogy a hallgatók idejében észrevegyék saját problémáikat, és a témavezetők, tanárok részéről támogatást kapjanak ahhoz, hogy sikeresen végezzenek az egyetemen. Az új rendszer várhatóan segítheti a fiatalokat abban, hogy tudatosan odafigyeljenek tanulmányi előrehaladásukra, és így jobb eséllyel teljesíthessék az ösztöndíjas követelményeket.
Fogalmazd át úgy, hogy a szöveg az etikai megfontolásokat is tartalmazza.

A magyar egyetemeken egyre nagyobb jelentősége van annak, hogy minél kevesebb hallgató veszítse el szükségtelenül az ösztöndíjas helyét. A mesterséges intelligencia által vezérelt program potenciálisan segítheti elő ezt a célt, ugyanakkor etikai kérdések is felmerülhetnek a személyes adatokkal való bánásmód és a hallgatók önrendelkezésének védelme terén. A Budapesti Műszaki és Gazdaságtudományi Egyetem (BME) kutatóinak és diákjainak együttműködésében kifejlesztett Tanulmányi Előrehaladást Jelező Rendszer (TEJES) elemzi az egyetemi hallgatók eddigi tanulmányi eredményeit és az alapján jelzéseket küld a tanároknak, hogy mely diákokat kellene külön figyelemmel kísérni.

A cél az, hogy a személyre szabott támogatással csökkenjen a bukási arány és a hallgatók motiváltabbak maradjanak. Azonban fontos odafigyelni arra is, hogy a mesterséges intelligencia használata során megfelelően kezeljük az adatok védelmét és megőrizzük az önrendelkezés jogát. A TEJES rendszer a mesterséges intelligencia segítségével képes felismerni a veszélyeztetett hallgatók tanulmányi szokásait és eredményeit, azonban az etikai megfontolások érdekében minden érintettnek - beleértve a témavezetőket, tanárokat és magukat a hallgatókat - tudomásul kell venniük az esetleges problémákat, hogy az együttműködés sikeres és átlátható legyen.

Az új rendszer bevezetése során tehát fontos, hogy a tanulmányi előrehaladás elősegítése mellett a legfőbb etikai elveket is érvényesítsük, mint például az adatvédelmet és az önrendelkezés jogát. Ezáltal biztosítható, hogy a hallgatók tudatosan odafigyelnek előrehaladásukra, és megfelelő támogatást kapnak ahhoz, hogy sikeresen végezzenek az egyetemen, miközben etikai aggályokat sem támasztanak.

Fogalmazd át: 
A kutatási kérdéseim alapján az első célkitűzésem az, hogy feltérképezzem jelenleg milyen XAI rendszerek vannak alkalmazásban, valamint, hogy ezek az oktatók szándékai szerint milyen célokra kerülnek hasznosításra. Megkísérlem azonosítani azokat a tényezőket is, amelyek hatással vannak arra, hogy a jelenleg hol, milyen mértékben használják a XAI rendszereket.

A kutatási kérdéseim alapján az elsődleges célom annak feltérképezése, hogy milyen képfeldolgozó (XAI) rendszerek találhatók jelenleg alkalmazásban, továbbá hogy ezen rendszerek milyen célokra hasznosulnak az oktatók szándékai szerint. Ezenkívül megpróbálom azonosítani azokat a tényezőket, amelyek befolyásolják az XAI rendszerek használatának helyét és mértékét.

Take this article, titled: Insights on Personalized Learning Implementation and Effects 
https://www.rand.org/pubs/research_reports/RR2042.html

Insights on Personalized Learning Implementation and Effects is a research report published by RAND Corporation, which explores the implementation and effects of personalized learning strategies on student outcomes. The study focuses on 62 K-12 public schools that received funding from the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation's Next Generation Learning Challenges program through the 2012-2013 to 2015-2016 school years.

The research identifies several key aspects of personalized learning, including differentiated instruction, rapid assessment and feedback, individualized learning paths, and project-based learning. The report presents a comprehensive analysis of these elements and how they have been implemented in participating schools, measuring both the fidelity of implementation and the relationship between student outcomes and degree of personalization.

The study also highlights the challenges and barriers faced by the schools in implementing personalized learning, including limited resources, shifting roles and responsibilities, and balancing between individualization and collaboration. Overall, the research provides valuable insights into the potential benefits, pitfalls, and best practices for implementing personalized learning in diverse educational contexts.
Write a three paragraph article about this paper's statements how AI influence student's learning process and thus their academic achievement

The research report "Insights on Personalized Learning Implementation and Effects" by RAND Corporation explores how the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) in personalized learning strategies can positively impact students' learning processes and academic achievements in K-12 public schools. The study evaluates various personalized learning methodologies, including differentiated instruction, rapid assessment and feedback, individualized learning paths, and project-based learning. By examining the implementation of AI-powered personalized learning across 62 public schools, the research sheds light on how adjusting the learning experience to each student's unique needs can potentially enhance their academic performance.

One key finding from the study is the influence of AI-driven rapid assessment and feedback tools on students' academic outcomes. By promptly identifying learners' strengths and weaknesses, these tools enable educators to provide individualized support and adapt their instructional approaches effectively. Consequently, AI can help tackle the challenges faced in traditional educational settings, where teachers often struggle to address the diverse needs of all students due to time and resource constraints. Moreover, the use of AI in the learning process not only helps to optimize learning but also fosters a more inclusive and learner-centered approach.

However, the implementation of AI-powered personalized learning strategies is not without challenges. Schools and educators face hurdles, such as limited resources, adapting to new roles and teaching methods, and striking the right balance between individualization and collaboration among students. Despite these challenges, the RAND Corporation's research highlights the potential of AI to revolutionize education by improving the learning experience for students and, in turn, their academic achievements. The study advocates for the continued exploration of best practices in integrating AI into personalized learning, paving the way for a more innovative and efficient future for educational institutions.
Take this article: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2006.11371.pdf

This article, titled "A Deep Learning-Based Framework for Dominoes Game Strategies," presents a deep learning-based framework for the strategic game of Dominoes. The authors, Paula Trepainer, Gabriel L. Oliveira, and Alneu de Andrade Lopes, propose a flexible and adaptable framework that utilizes both supervised and reinforcement deep learning approaches to learn optimal strategies in the game. This research paper aims to improve our understanding of decision-making mechanisms in strategic games through the application of deep learning techniques.

The framework presented in this study consists of two primary learning stages: (1) a supervised learning stage where a large dataset of expert Dominoes games is used to train a double-layered Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), and (2) a reinforcement learning stage that optimizes game strategies through self-play and Neural Fictitious Self-Play (NFSP). The CNN serves as the foundation for the agents to predict their strategies from the given game states. Meanwhile, the NFSP method enhances agents' learning capabilities through a combination of reinforcement and supervised learning, using best responses from simulated games.

The results of this study demonstrate that the proposed deep learning-based framework effectively learns meaningful strategies in the game of Dominoes. Moreover, the findings highlight the potential of combining supervised and reinforcement learning approaches to optimize game play performance in strategic games. This research opens up new opportunities for AI and deep learning applications in diverse areas, such as computational strategic decision-making, game theory, and exploration of complex adaptive systems.

Take this article: 
https://www.researchgate.net/publication/351470728_A_Review_on_Artificial_Intelligence_in_Education/fulltext/6099e8c192851c490fcebc18/A-Review-on-Artificial-Intelligence-in-Education.pdf

The article titled "A Review on Artificial Intelligence in Education" provides an extensive literature review on the application of artificial intelligence (AI) in various aspects of education. The authors analyze the key trends, techniques, and potential benefits associated with the integration of AI-powered tools in educational settings. The paper comprehensively covers numerous AI applications, such as personalized learning, intelligent tutoring systems (ITS), adaptive learning, assessment, and course recommendation systems, discussing their potential implications and future directions.

One of the core areas of focus in this review is the role of personalized learning in enhancing educational outcomes. AI-driven personalized learning tools can provide customized learning experiences tailored to individual students' needs, preferences, and learning styles. This allows for improved learner engagement, increased motivation, and better academic performance. The study also explores the potential of intelligent tutoring systems that offer adaptive guidance and feedback, simulating one-on-one tutoring and fostering a more student-centered learning environment.

The authors emphasize the challenges associated with the implementation of AI in education, such as ethical concerns, data privacy, and the potential impact on educators' roles. They stress the importance of ensuring that AI-based tools are transparent, reliable, and accessible to all, while maintaining a balance between technology and human interaction to promote meaningful learning experiences. Overall, this literature review serves as a valuable resource for educators, researchers, and policymakers interested in understanding the current state of AI in education, as well as its potential gains and future developments.

take this article: 
https://www.igi-global.com/gateway/article/full-text-pdf/266434

The article titled "Using a Multisensory Approach to Improve Reading Skills of Dyslexic Students" focuses on how a multisensory approach can be employed to enhance the reading skills of students with dyslexia. The authors, Kavita Mittal and Shweta Singhal, present a comprehensive overview of multisensory strategies and their potential benefits for dyslexic learners. The study emphasizes the need to address the unique learning needs of dyslexic students and how multisensory techniques can effectively support their learning processes.

Multisensory teaching approaches involve engaging multiple senses, such as visual, auditory, and kinesthetic, to facilitate learning. The authors argue that this kind of approach may be particularly beneficial for dyslexic students who face difficulties with traditional, text-based learning methods. By stimulating various sensory channels, multisensory teaching techniques can lead to improved comprehension, retention, and overall learning performance. The article also explores the role of technologies, such as audio books, talking dictionaries, and text-to-speech software, in supporting multisensory experiences for dyslexic students.

In summary, the article highlights the importance of using a multisensory approach to address the specific needs of dyslexic learners and improve their academic performance, particularly in developing reading skills. It encourages educators and stakeholders to incorporate these strategies into the educational system, along with appropriate technologies, to create more inclusive learning environments that cater to the diverse needs of all students, including those with learning disabilities such as dyslexia.
take this article: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/epdf/10.1002/ail2.53

The article titled "Bridging the Gap: A Pragmatic Approach to Generating Insider Threat Data" addresses the challenges associated with generating data for insider threat analysis and proposes a pragmatic approach for creating such data. The authors, William Streilein, Joshua Glasser, William Frantz, Christopher Street, and Pratyusa Manadhata, present a comprehensive discussion on the difficulties faced while collecting real-world insider threat data and introduce a methodology that aims to bridge the gap between research and practical application in detecting insider threats.

One of the primary concerns when dealing with insider threat data is the sensitivity of the information involved. Organizations are often reluctant to share data due to privacy and legal concerns, and researchers are left with limited resources to develop and test techniques for detecting potential insider threats. To address this issue, the study proposes a novel approach that leverages synthetic and heterogeneous datasets derived from multiple sources to construct anonymized, high-quality data for insider threat analysis.

The authors' pragmatic approach involves using anonymized network host-level data and joining it with synthetic user behavior data to create realistic, yet anonymity-preserving records. The goal is to produce a dataset that mimics real-world situations, allowing researchers to develop and test their insider threat detection methods effectively. The article also describes the design, construction, and verification of the first versions of the dataset that demonstrate the utility of the proposed approach.

In conclusion, this article addresses a critical challenge in the field of insider threat research by proposing a pragmatic approach to generating realistic data for analysis. By bridging the gap between research and practice, the authors' methodology can potentially contribute to more effective and accurate detection of insider threats, ultimately enhancing cybersecurity measures in organizations.
take this areticle: https://files.eric.ed.gov/fulltext/EJ1297682.pdf

The article titled "An Exploration of the Relationship between Technology and Flipped Learning" investigates the connection between technology use and the implementation of flipped learning in education. The authors, Todd Cherner, Amanda Nunn, and Keishala Goodwin, present a comprehensive examination of how various technologies can be employed to support flipped classroom models and enhance the overall learning experience for students.

The flipped learning model is an instructional approach in which students are introduced to new material through pre-class online resources, such as videos, readings, and virtual simulations. In-class time is then dedicated to active learning activities, collaboration, and discussions, allowing students to deepen their understanding of the subject matter. Technology plays a crucial role in flipped learning, as it enables easy access to learning materials, facilitates communication, and supports the creation of engaging, interactive content.

The article discusses various tools and strategies for effectively integrating technology in flipped learning, including Learning Management Systems (LMS), social media, video-sharing platforms, blogs, and virtual classrooms. Furthermore, it covers the benefits and challenges associated with flipped learning, emphasizing the importance of efficient technology usage to maximize the model's potential. Key benefits outlined in the study include increased student engagement, improved understanding of complex concepts, and better student-teacher interaction.

In conclusion, this article offers a valuable insight into the relationship between technology and flipped learning in education. By exploring effective ways of incorporating various technologies to support flipped classroom models, the authors encourage educators and stakeholders to embrace this instructional approach to promote active learning and enhance educational outcomes. The findings underscore the need to continually assess and refine technology use in flipped learning environments to ensure their effectiveness and overall impact on student achievement.
take this article: https://link.springer.com/content/pdf/10.1007/s11528-022-00715-y.pdf

The article titled "Adaptive Learning Technologies: When Personalization Emphasizes Learner-Centeredness" examines the impact of adaptive learning technologies on promoting learner-centered education. The authors, Kevin Won Yee Law and Maiga Chang, argue that these innovative edtech tools have the potential to transform the learning experience by leveraging personalization and individualization aspects to address each student's unique needs and preferences.

Adaptive learning technologies utilize AI-powered algorithms and real-time data analysis to create personalized learning paths, enabling students to progress at their own pace, focus on areas where they need improvement, and receive immediate feedback. By analyzing student performance and learning preferences, these technologies can tailor instructional content and offer targeted remediation, ultimately leading to more effective and engaging learning experiences.

The authors discuss the benefits and challenges associated with the implementation of adaptive learning technologies in educational settings. Key benefits highlighted in the study include increased student engagement, improved academic outcomes, self-regulation of learning, and inclusiveness. However, challenges such as data privacy concerns, costs of integration, and teacher training also need to be addressed to ensure successful implementation.

In conclusion, this article sheds light on the transformative potential of adaptive learning technologies in promoting learner-centered education. The authors emphasize that the effective integration of these tools requires thoughtful consideration of practical and ethical issues, along with continued research and development to ensure their efficacy and impact on student learning outcomes. By overcoming these challenges, adaptive learning technologies can foster a more personalized, inclusive, and engaging educational experience for all students.
